algorithms.title: Geriatric Acute Confusion Field Approach
algorithms.description: Sequence for evaluating a geriatric patient with new or worsening confusion.
steps: Establish the patient's cognitive/functional baseline from a caregiver or family member — do not assume based on presentation to an unfamiliar provider alone.
Determine whether the confusion is new or represents an acute change from that baseline.
If new or acutely changed: treat as delirium until proven otherwise.

Search for reversible causes: infection (UTI, pneumonia), medication effects/polypharmacy, hypoxia, electrolyte disturbance, dehydration, urinary retention/constipation, pain, unfamiliar environment/sensory deprivation.
Consider atypical presentations of serious underlying conditions (silent MI, afebrile infection, painless acute abdomen) even when symptoms seem mild.
Transport for evaluation; do not dismiss acute confusion as simply dementia progression or normal aging in the field.
